reasoning: A US invasion of Iran to establish territorial control is highly improbable. Historically, US military operations against Iran have been restricted to proportional airstrikes, maritime skirmishes, and cyber actions rather than land invasions. An invasion would require enormous logistical buildup, troop mobilization (hundreds of thousands of soldiers), and legislative or executive momentum that would take months to organize and would draw massive international and domestic attention. Given that the knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025, there are only about 7 weeks remaining before the December 31 deadline. Since no such immense mobilization was a definitive global reality prior to this date (as reflected by the 3% market price in late October 2025), a sudden invasion within this narrow window is essentially impossible. The 3% probability efficiently captures the baseline noise/black swan risk on prediction platforms for extreme events with short time horizons.
